hero.badge: Real zero-knowledge · Encrypted on your device · Open source
hero.titleLine2: Encrypted before it leaves your device.
hero.subtitle: Most clouds promise not to look at your files. Noctcom is built so that it can't, even if we wanted to. Your password and your keys never touch our servers. We only store encrypted data we can't open.

zk.title: Zero-knowledge by default
zk.body: Not a setting you turn on: it's the only way it works. Argon2id + XChaCha20-Poly1305.

metadata.title: Not even metadata
metadata.body: Names, sizes and tags are encrypted too. Not just the content.

email.title: No email in the clear
email.body: We don't even store your email: just a hash. A database dump won't reveal who you are.

share.title: Anonymous sharing
share.body: X25519 sealed boxes: only the recipient opens the file. Not us, not anyone with server access.

demo.title: Don't take our word for it: <hl>watch it</hl>.
demo.subtitle: The full security walkthrough in 84 seconds: what happens to your password, what travels over the network, and what our own server can (and can't) see.

notDo.title: What we <hl>don't</hl> do.
notDo.body: Noctcom is not an office suite. There's no chat, no video calls, no online document editor. It's deliberate: every feature that gives a server the ability to read your data is a crack in your privacy. We do one thing —store your files so that only you can read them— and we do it better than anyone.
notDo.note: Algorithm versioning ready for post-quantum (Kyber/Dilithium on the roadmap). Your encryption today won't expire tomorrow.

audience.title: For those to whom privacy <hl>isn't optional</hl>.
audience.subtitle: If you handle material that can't leak —an investigation, a source, a case file— you need more than a provider's promise to «not look». You need it to be unable to.

journalism.title: Investigative journalism
journalism.body: Keep sensitive material encrypted on your device before it ever leaves it. Not even we can open it.

sources.title: Sources and whistleblowing
sources.body: Share with your newsroom via X25519 sealed boxes: only the recipient opens it, not even with server access.

professional.title: Professional confidentiality
professional.body: Lawyers, doctors, journalists: client, patient or source data with encryption the provider can't break.

jurisdiction.title: Control and jurisdiction
jurisdiction.body: AGPL self-host: your data on your own hardware, outside other people's jurisdictions and providers.

audience.roadmapNote: On the roadmap — verifiable chain of custody: authorship signing and timestamping to prove to an editor or a judge that a file is intact and from a given date, without anyone else reading it. All built on hashes and signatures; the content never leaves your control.
audience.honestyNote: Honesty first: zero-knowledge encryption protects your files at rest, it doesn't give you network anonymity. To hide who you talk to, combine it with tools like Tor according to your threat model. And like everything in Noctcom, the code is open and auditable.

selfhost.title: Your server, your rules.
selfhost.body: Noctcom is 100% open source (AGPL-3.0). Deploy it on your own hardware with a single Docker command. Same encryption, no third parties. Your cloud, your hardware, real zero-knowledge.

control.title: Total control
control.body: Your data on your infrastructure. No middlemen, no external jurisdictions.

auditable.title: Auditable
auditable.body: Open source AGPL-3.0. Verify every line that protects your files.

sameCrypto.title: Same encryption
sameCrypto.body: Identical cryptographic guarantees as the cloud version. Real zero-knowledge.
